stimulus: Many young plants lose chlorophyll when exposed to streptomycin, an antibiotic produced by certain soil bacteria. Howard University botanist Dorian Akinyi and her colleagues hypothesized that this response involves a change in how the plants take up minerals such as calcium and magnesium. The researchers grew two groups of Brassica rapa seedlings, one exposed to streptomycin and one a control group without exposure, and measured the seedlings’ mineral content seven days after germination.
stem: Which choice best describes data in the table that support Akinyi and her colleagues’ hypothesis?

answerOptions.content: The control seedlings contained higher levels of magnesium than calcium, but the seedlings exposed to streptomycin contained higher levels of calcium than magnesium.
Both groups of seedlings contained more than 100 mg/kg of both calcium and magnesium.
Calcium levels were around 180 mg/kg in the control seedlings but nearly 520 mg/kg in the seedlings exposed to streptomycin.
The seedlings exposed to streptomycin showed lower levels of calcium and magnesium than the control seedlings did.

rationale: Choice D is the best answer. The hypothesis is that streptomycin exposure alters mineral uptake. The table shows that streptomycin-exposed seedlings had lower calcium (180 vs. 520) and lower magnesium (220 vs. 340) than controls, supporting altered uptake. Choice A is incorrect because the control seedlings actually had higher calcium than magnesium, not the reverse, and the streptomycin group also had higher calcium than magnesium is false (180 < 220), so the description is doubly wrong. Choice B is incorrect because, while accurate, it does not contrast the two groups and so does not bear on the hypothesis. Choice C is incorrect because it inverts the actual control and treated values.
